# Gathering Guide

The gathering loop is the entry profession. A prospector surveys the terrain,
harvests deposits with the harvester tool, and refines raw ore into ingots that
crafters buy. This guide fixes the vocabulary every other system references.

## Survey Tool

The survey tool paints a heatmap of subsurface deposits before you commit a
single harvest. A dense reading means a rich node sits nearby; a faint reading
means the deposit is thin or already exhausted. Surveying spends a little energy
but saves a great many wasted swings on a poor node, so a careful prospector
always surveys before harvesting an unfamiliar patch of terrain.

## Yield Modifier

Yield is the amount of raw resource a single harvest action returns. The yield
modifier stacks additively from the tool tier and multiplicatively from the node
richness, so a tier-three harvester on a rich node returns far more ore per swing
than a tier-one harvester on a poor node. Yield never depends on the player
character level directly; it is entirely a function of the tool tier and the node
richness, and nothing about experience or class changes that relationship.

## Resource Quality Tiers

Resource quality is rolled per harvest across five named tiers, from lowest to
highest: crude, common, fine, pristine, and flawless. The roll is drawn against
the node's quality band, so even a rich node occasionally returns a crude result
and even a poor node can, rarely, return something pristine. Higher tiers gate
which recipes a crafter may attempt, and flawless stock is scarce enough that it
commands the steepest markup in the whole economy.

## Upgrade Path

Upgrading the harvester consumes credits and refined ingots at every tier. Each
upgrade widens the node richness the tool can profitably work and nudges the
quality band it rolls against slightly upward. The upgrade path is deliberately
long and expensive so the gatherer economy and the crafter economy stay locked
in interdependence across the entire progression rather than decoupling early.

## Quick Reference

- yield: tool tier plus node richness, never character level
- quality: crude, common, fine, pristine, flawless — premium on the top two
- node: richness sets both yield and the quality band
- upgrade: credits and ingots, tier by tier
- survey: read node richness before you harvest
